issues.message: Form control is missing an accessible label

evidence.reason: Form control has no accessible label.
evidence.name: student_name

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible <label> connected with for/id. Use aria-label only when a visible label is not possible.

rule.title: Form control missing accessible label
rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the visual design already implies the label but fails programmatically.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot always confirm labels created dynamically by JavaScript.

issues.message: Form control is missing an accessible label

evidence.reason: Form control has no accessible label.
evidence.name: accommodation_request

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible <label> connected with for/id. Use aria-label only when a visible label is not possible.

rule.title: Form control missing accessible label
rule.why_it_matters: Students using keyboard navigation, assistive technology, or simplified layouts may not know what information the field expects.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the visual design already implies the label but fails programmatically.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot always confirm labels created dynamically by JavaScript.

evidence.reason: Link text is generic and does not explain the destination or action.

issues.suggested_fix: Use descriptive link text like "Download scholarship guidelines" instead of "click here."

rule.title: Link text is too generic
rule.why_it_matters: Students who navigate by a list of links hear 'click here' with no context and cannot tell links apart.
rule.manual_review_notes: Surrounding text may add context visually, but the link should still make sense on its own.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check only matches a small list of common generic phrases.

issues.message: Button is missing an accessible name

evidence.reason: Button has no visible text, aria-label, title, or image alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add clear button text or an aria-label that describes the button action.

rule.title: Button missing accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A button without a name is announced as just 'button' by screen readers, so students cannot tell what pressing it will do.
rule.manual_review_notes: Icon-only buttons may look meaningful visually but still need a programmatic name.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static HTML cannot see names added later by JavaScript or icon fonts.

issues.message: Image is missing useful alt text

evidence.reason: Image is missing useful alt text.

issues.suggested_fix: Add alt text that describes the image purpose, or mark decorative images as presentation.

rule.title: Image missing useful alt text
rule.why_it_matters: Students using screen readers miss the image content entirely, which can hide instructions, diagrams, or required information.
rule.manual_review_notes: Confirm whether the image is informative or decorative; only a human can judge if existing alt text is actually useful.
rule.static_check_limitations: Static checks cannot judge alt text quality, only whether it exists.

evidence.reason: Heading level jumps from h1 to h3.

issues.suggested_fix: Do not skip heading levels; move from h1 to h2 before h3.

rule.title: Heading level is skipped
rule.why_it_matters: Jumping from h1 to h3 breaks the page outline that screen reader users rely on to understand and navigate content.
rule.manual_review_notes: Check whether the heading order still makes sense when read as an outline.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check follows document order only; visual layout may differ.

evidence.reason: Video has no captions or subtitles track.

issues.suggested_fix: Add captions or subtitles for education video content.

rule.title: Video missing captions
rule.why_it_matters: Deaf and hard-of-hearing students cannot access spoken lesson content without captions or subtitles.
rule.manual_review_notes: Captions may be provided by an embedded player or platform instead of a track element.
rule.static_check_limitations: The check only sees <track> elements; player-level or burned-in captions are invisible to static HTML.

issues.message: Focused control has no accessible name

evidence.reason: A keyboard user reached this control, but it has no label, text, aria-label, or other usable name.

issues.suggested_fix: Add a visible label, text content, or aria-label so students know what the control does.

rule.title: Focused control has no accessible name
rule.why_it_matters: A keyboard user can reach the control, but without a name a screen reader announces nothing useful about what it does.
rule.manual_review_notes: The accessible name is estimated; confirm with browser dev tools or a screen reader what is actually announced.
rule.browser_check_limitations: Accessible names are estimated from labels, text, and common attributes, not from a full accessibility tree computation.

limitations: This prototype runs static HTML checks plus a basic keyboard interaction audit.
It does not replace a full human accessibility audit.
Browser mode does not simulate a full screen reader, and accessible names are estimated.

likely_blockers.message: Form control is missing an accessible label
likely_blockers.task_impact: Form control may be hard to understand or complete because it has no accessible label.

likely_blockers.message: Form control is missing an accessible label
likely_blockers.task_impact: Form control may be hard to understand or complete because it has no accessible label.
Link text may not clearly explain the destination or action.

likely_blockers.message: Button is missing an accessible name
likely_blockers.task_impact: Student may not be able to identify what an unlabeled button does.
Student may struggle to understand the page structure and task sections.

focus_trace.name: school_name
focus_trace.text: Choose a school Riverside High School Hillview Community School

limitations: Browser mode approximates keyboard interaction but does not simulate a full screen reader.
Accessible names are estimated and require manual review.
